# Boggart Boneboiler

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Boneboiler |
| **Description** | A bloated, greasy humanoid with slick skin and fat folds glistening under firelight. It reeks of burnt fat, spoiled meat, and sour sweat. Clad in a stained leather apron stretched tight over its bulk, it lumbers about with wet, slapping steps. Its small, beady eyes gleam with cruel delight as it tends to bubbling pots filled with bones and scraps, occasionally dragging victims closer to “the boil.” |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (leather apron) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 + 2 |
| **Strength** | 12 (+1) |
| **Constitution** | 12 (+1) |
| **Agility** | 10 (+0) |
| **Intellect** | 8 (-1) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 10 (+0) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Meat Hook:** +3 to hit, range 1, 1d6 piercing; target suffers **Bleeding** (1d4 damage at the start of its next turn). Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler lunges at (insert target) with a meat hook!* **Hurl Hot Grease:** +2 to hit, range 3, 2d4 fire damage; **recharge 6** (roll a d6 at the start of its turn and regain use on a 6); target is **Blinded** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler hurls a pot of hot grease at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Polished Finger Bone** (15%) |
| **Comments** | “Into the pot with ya!”, “Boil ’em slow, tastes better!”, “Oi, don’t waste the juices!”, “This one’ll make a fine broth!”, “Keep stirrin’, lads!”, “Boss wants this one salted!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Boneboiler has a chance to drop:
- **Polished Finger Bone** — 15%

### Polished Finger Bone Summary
A smooth, yellowed finger bone, carefully cleaned and buffed to a dull shine.
- non-magical
- worth 25 gp

### XP Note
The submitted stat block listed **25 XP**, but this entry uses **50 XP** to match the current Umberhold experience table for a **Level 2** monster.

content.text:
# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

# Boggart Knifer

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Knifer |
| **Description** | A small, wiry humanoid with patchy, grimy skin and oversized ears. Its eyes gleam with sly hunger, and its yellowed teeth jut out in a crooked grin. It smells of damp earth, sour sweat, and old blood. Clad in mismatched scraps of leather and cloth, it creeps and twitches constantly, muttering under its breath and circling foes like a scavenger sizing up a carcass. |
| **Level** | 1 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 13 (base 10 + Agility + leather armor) |
| **Hit Points** | 1d6 |
| **Strength** | 10 (+0) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 12 (+1) |
| **Intellect** | 8 (-1) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 8 (-1) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Agility +3 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | Stealth +3 |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Rusty Dagger:** +3 to hit, range 1, 1d4 piercing. Combat text: *Boggart Knifer stabs at (insert target) with a rusty dagger!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Boggart Cloak** (1 in 500) |
| **Comments** | “Get stuck in, lads!”, “Oi, this one’s got meat on ’em!”, “Stab it in the hammy so it can’t run!”, “Heh, I’m keepin’ the good bits!”, “Don’t break it—boss said fresh!” |
| **Experience** | 25 |

---

## Notes

### Unique Rare Drop
In addition to normal loot, a Boggart Knifer has a rare chance to drop:
- **Boggart Cloak** — 1 in 500

### Boggart Cloak Summary
A patchwork cloak of filthy cloth, flayed skin, and scraps of wulf fur, reeking of damp rot.
- non-magical
- grants advantage on Stealth checks
- weight: 1 stone

# Wulf

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Wulf |
| **Description** | A small, mangy beast with patchy fur and a hunched, underfed frame. Its ribs press against its skin, and its eyes glint with mean, hungry intelligence. Its fangs are yellowed and rotten, and its breath is foul with the stench of decay. It reeks of wet fur, rot, and filth. These creatures skitter and snap in packs, yapping and circling like vermin, often driven and controlled by their Boggart masters, darting in to bite before retreating. |
| **Level** | 1 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |

A crude bone collar carved by Boggart masters, etched with jagged symbols.

I think I would create a boneboiler file by copying it into the new location, then clone the template for the other monsters while updating their names. There's no instruction on filling stat blocks, so I might just keep the template unchanged for now. Since the user likely wants a file for each existing monster, I could convert the current monster files into 5E versions."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering monster file organization**

I'm noticing that all the current monster files have the same structure, so it sounds reasonable to create folders and copy the existing markdown files into these new directories. I could set up a 5E folder with subfolders for each monster and place their respective files inside. However, if I use "boneboiler" as a template, the stats for "boggart-knifer" and "wulf" might be lost. It seems like the user just wants the new folders and files with the same content as before."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Planning monster folder structure**

I want to create a new folder structure under `umberhold/monsters/5E/`. For each existing monster markdown file in `umberhold/monsters/` (except the README), I'll create a directory using the slug that matches the filename without its extension. Inside each directory, I’ll make a markdown file keeping the original name, taking the content from the source. Since "boneboiler" is the template style, it might be useful to include a README, but I'll ensure it's not part of the items being copied."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering file management techniques**

I’m thinking about creating directories and files, and using bash commands like mkdir and cp sounds good. The developer suggests using read instead of cat or sed for reading. Bash is definitely okay for file operations like ls, rg, and find. It might be better to stick with bash for creating directories and managing copies. I’ll need to mention clear paths and consider preserving existing files. Let's execute this!"}]}

content.name: bash

arguments.command:
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p umberhold/monsters/5E
for file in umberhold/monsters/*.md; do
  base=$(basename "$file" .md)
  if [ "$base" = "README" ]; then
    continue
  fi
  mkdir -p "umberhold/monsters/5E/$base"
  cp "$file" "umberhold/monsters/5E/$base/$base.md"
done
find umberhold/monsters/5E -maxdepth 2 -type f | sort

# Umberhold Monster Stat Block Canon

This document captures the current approved generic monster stat block for Umberhold.

It defines the baseline fields used to build monster entries.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. Core Monster Fields

| Field | Details |
|---|---|
| Name | Name of the creature |
| Description | Describes the creature in detail, including appearance, odor, demeanor, behavior, and other sensory notes |
| Level | 1-20 |
| Size | Small, Medium, Large, Huge, or Gigantic |
| Type | Beast, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Insect, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, or Undead |
| Alignment | Good, Neutral, or Evil |
| AC | Base 10 when unarmored, modified by Agility, armor, and other effects |
| Hit Points | Determined by size and level |
| Strength | 3-30 |
| Constitution | 3-30 |
| Agility | 3-30 |
| Intellect | 3-30 |
| Piety | 3-30 |
| Personality | 3-30 |
| Luck | 3-30 |
| Proficiency Bonus | Based on level progression |
| Saving Throw Proficiencies | List of proficient saves |
| Skill Proficiencies | List of proficient skills |
| Damage Vulnerabilities | List of damage vulnerabilities |
| Damage Resistances | List of damage resistances |
| Damage Immunities | List of damage immunities |
| Condition Immunities | List of condition immunities |
| Attacks | List of attacks, including to-hit, damage, damage type, and special effects |
| Treasure Type | Treasure category from A-Z, based on treasure tables |
| Comments | Optional creature comments, including a 5% chance at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type |
| Experience | XP awarded to the party for slaying the monster |

---

## 2. Monster Level

Monsters use a **Level** value rather than an external challenge rating style label.

Current monster level range:
- **1-20**

---

## 3. Monster Size

Monster size categories are:
- Small
- Medium
- Large
- Huge
- Gigantic

### Combat Occupancy Mapping
Under Umberhold's current combat system, size maps to enemy rank occupancy as follows:
- Small = 1 slot
- Medium = 1 slot
- Large = 2 slots
- Huge = 3 slots
- Gigantic = 4 slots

---

## 4. Monster Types

The current approved monster types are:
- Beast
- Dragon
- Elemental
- Fey
- Fiend
- Giant
- Humanoid
- Insect
- Monstrosity
- Ooze
- Plant
- Undead

---

## 5. Alignment

The current approved alignment values are:
- Good
- Neutral
- Evil

---

## 6. Armor Class

Monster AC uses a base of **10** when unarmored.

AC may then be modified by:
- armor
- Agility bonus
- magical effects
- other special modifiers

---

## 7. Hit Point Progression by Size

Monster hit points are based on size.

| Size | Hit Dice per Level |
|---|---|
| Small | 1d6 per level |
| Medium | 1d8 per level |
| Large | 1d12 per level |
| Huge | 2d10 per level |
| Gigantic | 2d20 per level |

---

## 8. Core Attributes

All monsters use the same seven core attributes as player characters:
- Strength
- Constitution
- Agility
- Intellect
- Piety
- Personality
- Luck

Each attribute currently ranges from:
- **3-30**

---

## 9. Proficiency Bonus by Level

| Level Range | Proficiency Bonus |
|---|---|
| 1-3 | +2 |
| 4-6 | +3 |
| 7-9 | +4 |
| 10-12 | +5 |
| 13-15 | +6 |
| 16-18 | +7 |
| 19-20 | +8 |

---

## 10. Saving Throw and Skill Proficiencies

Monsters may have:
- saving throw proficiencies
- skill proficiencies

If a monster is proficient in a save or skill, it adds its proficiency bonus to the relevant roll.

---

## 11. Damage and Condition Defenses

Monster entries may include:
- damage vulnerabilities
- damage resistances
- damage immunities
- condition immunities

These should use Umberhold's current damage types and condition list.

---

## 12. Attacks

A monster's attack list should include, at minimum:
- attack name
- to-hit bonus
- damage
- damage type
- range
- any attached effects or conditions

### Attack Range Rule
Every monster attack must have a **range value from 1-3**.

---

## 13. Treasure, Comments, and Experience

### Treasure Type
Each monster may include a **Treasure Type** field from **A-Z**, based on Umberhold's treasure tables.

### Comments
Each monster may include a **Comments** field.

This currently supports flavor such as:
- a **5% chance** at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type

### Experience
Each monster should include an **Experience** value showing how much XP is awarded to the party for slaying it.

The exact XP values by monster level are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

---

## 14. Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- monsters use Level rather than challenge rating
- current monster levels are 1-20
- monsters use the five size categories Small, Medium, Large, Huge, and Gigantic
- Small and Medium occupy 1 combat slot
- Large occupies 2 combat slots
- Huge occupies 3 combat slots
- Gigantic occupies 4 combat slots
- the current monster type list is Beast, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Insect, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, and Undead
- the current alignment values are Good, Neutral, and Evil
- unarmored AC starts at 10
- hit point dice scale by size category
- monsters use the same seven core attributes as player characters
- monster attributes currently range from 3-30
- proficiency bonus follows the current level progression table
- monster entries include a Description field
- monster entries include saving throw proficiencies and skill proficiencies
- monster entries may include damage vulnerabilities, damage resistances, damage immunities, and condition immunities
- monster entries include attacks and XP value
- monster entries may include Treasure Type and Comments

---

## 15. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- whether monster hit points use only size dice or also add Constitution-derived bonuses by level
- whether alignment has any systemic effect beyond lore and spell interaction
- the full canonical skill list used by monsters and characters
- whether monster spellcasters follow player spell slot rules or use bespoke spellcasting packages

---

## 16. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

# Umberhold Experience Canon

This document captures the current approved rules for:
- monster XP values by monster level
- XP distribution to the party
- level-up access at inns

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. Experience Awards from Monsters

When a monster is slain, it grants experience based on its **monster level**.

### Experience by Monster Level

| Level | XP |
|---|---:|
| 1 | 25 |
| 2 | 50 |
| 3 | 100 |
| 4 | 200 |
| 5 | 400 |
| 6 | 600 |
| 7 | 800 |
| 8 | 1,200 |
| 9 | 1,600 |
| 10 | 2,400 |
| 11 | 3,200 |
| 12 | 4,200 |
| 13 | 5,400 |
| 14 | 6,800 |
| 15 | 8,500 |
| 16 | 10,500 |
| 17 | 13,000 |
| 18 | 16,000 |
| 19 | 19,500 |
| 20 | 23,500 |

---

## 2. XP Distribution

Experience gained by the party is:
- awarded when monsters are slain
- shared equally among **surviving party members**

Characters who are not surviving members at the end of the encounter do **not** receive a share of that encounter's experience.

---

## 3. Leveling Up

When a character has enough experience to advance to the next level:
- they must go to the nearest **inn**
- they choose the option to **advance**

### Level-Up Cost
- there is **no gold cost** to level up

### Note
The full level-up rules are still to be detailed separately.

---

## 4. Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- monsters award XP based on monster level
- the XP table for monster levels 1-20 is defined
- XP is shared equally among surviving party members
- leveling up is performed at an inn
- leveling up has no gold cost
- detailed level-up rules will be defined separately

---

## 5. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- the exact character XP thresholds required for levels 1-10
- whether benched roster members ever receive shared XP
- whether summoned allies or temporary NPC allies affect XP splitting
- whether story or quest XP exists in addition to monster XP
- whether escape or partial-victory outcomes grant reduced XP
- the exact level-up benefits by class and level

---

## 6. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

arguments.content:
# Boggart Gasser

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Gasser |
| **Description** | A stunted, pot-bellied humanoid swaddled in grime-stiff wrappings and a patchwork hood dark with old soot. Its slick skin is blotched with yellow lesions, and its nostrils are stuffed with greasy moss to blunt the fumes it brews. Glass vials clink from cords across its chest, each one sloshing with corpse-green vapor and rancid alchemical slurry. It reeks of rot, vinegar, burst lungs, and the sweet-sour taint of spoiled herbs left to steep in blood. When it grins, its blackened teeth show through cracked lips, and it cackles as it lobs choking death into clustered foes before scampering back into the murk. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 13 (base 10 + Agility + patchwork leathers) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 8 (-1) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 14 (+2) |
| **Intellect** | 10 (+0) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 8 (-1) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Agility +4 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | Stealth +4 |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |

| **Damage Immunities** | None |

Combat text: *Boggart Gasser darts in and jabs (insert target) with a jagged shank!* **Poison Gas Vial:** +4 to hit, range 3, 1d4 piercing + 1d4 poison; target must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or become **Poisoned** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser hurls a glass vial of poisonous gas at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Stoppered Gas Vial** (10%) |
| **Comments** | “Breathe deep, pretty thing!”, “Cough for me!”, “Rot in your chest, slow and wet!”, “Glass for the lungs, gas for the eyes!”, “Heh—listen to ’em choke!”, “Boss says don’t waste the good fumes!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Gasser has a chance to drop:
- **Stoppered Gas Vial** — 10%

### Stoppered Gas Vial Summary
A cloudy glass vial stoppered with black wax, still swirling with a foul green vapor that stings the eyes.
- non-magical
- worth 30 gp

### Tactics Note
Boggart Gassers prefer to fight from the rear ranks, hurling poison into weakened or tightly packed enemies while larger boggarts keep victims pinned in place.

arguments.content:
# Marrow Muncher

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Marrow Muncher |
| **Description** | A towering, thick-limbed brute with tangled, filthy hair and slabs of muscle beneath dirt-caked skin. Its gut hangs heavy over a crude belt strung with cracked skulls, trophies taken and later used as missiles. The creature reeks of unwashed flesh and old rot, its presence announced by stench before sight. Small, dim eyes peer out with dull hunger as it lumbers forward, dragging an oversized club scarred from countless beatings. |
| **Level** | 3 |
| **Size** | Large (2 squares on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Giant |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (base 10 + Agility -1 + thick hide) |
| **Hit Points** | 3d12 + 9 |
| **Strength** | 18 (+4) |
| **Constitution** | 17 (+3) |
| **Agility** | 8 (-1) |
| **Intellect** | 6 (-2) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 7 (-2) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Strength +6, Constitution +5 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Oversized Club:** +6 to hit, range 2, 1d10 + 4 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *The Marrow Muncher swings its massive club at (insert target)!* **Thrown Skull:** +6 to hit, range 3, 1d10 + 4 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *The Marrow Muncher hurls a skull from its grisly belt at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | C; **Skull Belt** (1 in 200) |
| **Comments** | “Smash you flat!”, “Bones mine—me keep!”, “Stop wrigglin’!”, “Heh... you break easy!”, “I eat after fight...” |
| **Experience** | 100 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Marrow Muncher has a rare chance to drop:
- **Skull Belt** — 1 in 200

### Skull Belt Summary
A crude belt strung with cracked and weathered skulls, still faintly reeking of death.
- non-magical
- grants advantage on Intimidation checks
- weight: 4 stone

content.text:
# Boggart Gasser

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Gasser |
| **Description** | A stunted, pot-bellied humanoid swaddled in grime-stiff wrappings and a patchwork hood dark with old soot. Its slick skin is blotched with yellow lesions, and its nostrils are stuffed with greasy moss to blunt the fumes it brews. Glass vials clink from cords across its chest, each one sloshing with corpse-green vapor and rancid alchemical slurry. It reeks of rot, vinegar, burst lungs, and the sweet-sour taint of spoiled herbs left to steep in blood. When it grins, its blackened teeth show through cracked lips, and it cackles as it lobs choking death into clustered foes before scampering back into the murk. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 13 (base 10 + Agility + patchwork leathers) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 8 (-1) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 14 (+2) |
| **Intellect** | 10 (+0) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 8 (-1) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Agility +4 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | Stealth +4 |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Jagged Shank:** +1 to hit, range 1, 1d4-1 piercing. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser darts in and jabs (insert target) with a jagged shank!* **Poison Gas Vial:** +4 to hit, range 3, 1d4 piercing + 1d4 poison; target must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or become **Poisoned** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser hurls a glass vial of poisonous gas at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Stoppered Gas Vial** (10%) |
| **Comments** | “Breathe deep, pretty thing!”, “Cough for me!”, “Rot in your chest, slow and wet!”, “Glass for the lungs, gas for the eyes!”, “Heh—listen to ’em choke!”, “Boss says don’t waste the good fumes!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Gasser has a chance to drop:
- **Stoppered Gas Vial** — 10%

### Stoppered Gas Vial Summary
A cloudy glass vial stoppered with black wax, still swirling with a foul green vapor that stings the eyes.
- non-magical
- worth 30 gp

### Tactics Note
Boggart Gassers prefer to fight from the rear ranks, hurling poison into weakened or tightly packed enemies while larger boggarts keep victims pinned in place.

If the save fails by 5 or more, the target is also **Blinded**. The target may repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending both conditions on a success. Combat text: *Boggart Gasser hurls a vial of choking rotgas at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Rotgas Vial** (10%) |
| **Comments** | “Breathe deep, pretty thing!”, “Cough for me!”, “Rot in your chest, slow and wet!”, “Gas for the lungs!”, “Heh—listen to ’em choke!”, “Don’t waste the good fumes!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Gasser has a chance to drop:
- **Rotgas Vial** — 10%

### Rotgas Vial Summary
A stoppered glass vial swirling with foul green vapor that burns the eyes and throat.
- non-magical
- thrown weapon
- as above
- single-use
- weight: 1 stone
"}

content.text:
# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Boggart Gasser](./boggart-gasser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Choking boggart bomb-thrower that lobs poisonous gas vials and may carry a **Rotgas Vial** |
| [Marrow Muncher](./marrow-muncher.md) | 3 | Large | Giant | Reeking brute that smashes foes with an oversized club and hurls skulls from its grisly belt |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

Her skin hangs loose and mottled, marked with boils and ritual cuts, and her breath carries the sour stink of rot and old bile. Bone charms and fetishes clatter softly at her waist as she mutters to herself, eyes gleaming with spiteful cunning. She croons half-formed lullabies before unleashing foul magic, delighting in watching her victims falter and fade. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (base 10 + Agility + filthy wrappings) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 8 (-1) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 12 (+1) |
| **Intellect** | 12 (+1) |
| **Piety** | 12 (+1) |
| **Personality** | 10 (+0) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Piety +3 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Crude Club:** +1 to hit, range 1, 1d6 - 1 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife swings a crude club at (insert target)!* **Venomspit (Cantrip):** +3 to hit, range 3, 2d4 acid damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife spits a glob of hissing venom at (insert target)!* **Rotwife’s Lullaby (Recharge 5/6):** range 3; one enemy and up to one adjacent ally on each side in the same rank (3 max); targets must make a **PIE save** or fall asleep. Each target may repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Ends immediately for a target if it takes damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife croons a foul lullaby at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | B; **Silver Unholy Symbol** (5%) |
| **Comments** | “Sleep now, little meat...”, “Close them eyes... never wake...”, “Heh... dream of rot...”, “Soft flesh, soft mind...”, “Hush now... hush...” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Rotwife has a chance to drop:
- **Silver Unholy Symbol** — 5%

### Silver Unholy Symbol Summary
A crude, tarnished icon etched with profane markings, sticky with old grime.
- mundane
- worth 25 gp

### Spell Note
**Rotwife’s Lullaby** affects one enemy and up to one adjacent ally on each side in the same rank, for a maximum of 3 targets. Sleeping targets may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns, and the effect ends immediately if a target takes damage.

She croons half-formed lullabies before unleashing foul magic, delighting in watching her victims falter and fade. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (base 10 + Agility + filthy wrappings) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 8 (-1) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 12 (+1) |
| **Intellect** | 12 (+1) |
| **Piety** | 12 (+1) |
| **Personality** | 10 (+0) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Piety +3 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Crude Club:** +1 to hit, range 1, 1d6 - 1 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife swings a crude club at (insert target)!* **Venomspit (Cantrip):** +3 to hit, range 3, 2d4 acid damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife spits a glob of hissing venom at (insert target)!* **Rotwife’s Lullaby (Recharge 5/6):** range 3; one enemy and up to one adjacent ally on each side in the same rank (3 max); targets must make a **PIE save** or fall asleep. Each target may repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Ends immediately for a target if it takes damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife croons a foul lullaby at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | B; **Silver Unholy Symbol** (5%) |
| **Comments** | “Sleep now, little meat...”, “Close them eyes... never wake...”, “Heh... dream of rot...”, “Soft flesh, soft mind...”, “Hush now... hush...” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Rotwife has a chance to drop:
- **Silver Unholy Symbol** — 5%

### Silver Unholy Symbol Summary
A crude, tarnished icon etched with profane markings, sticky with old grime.
- mundane
- worth 25 gp

### Spell Note
**Rotwife’s Lullaby** affects one enemy and up to one adjacent ally on each side in the same rank, for a maximum of 3 targets. Sleeping targets may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns, and the effect ends immediately if a target takes damage.
"}

content.text:
# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Boggart Gasser](./boggart-gasser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Choking boggart bomb-thrower that lobs poisonous gas vials and may carry a **Rotgas Vial** |
| [Boggart Rotwife](./boggart-rotwife.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Filthy boggart crone that spits venom and croons a sleep-inducing **Rotwife’s Lullaby** |
| [Marrow Muncher](./marrow-muncher.md) | 3 | Large | Giant | Reeking brute that smashes foes with an oversized club and hurls skulls from its grisly belt |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

# Boggart Bruiser

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Bruiser |
| **Description** | A squat, thick-shouldered boggart clad in a rust-choked chain shirt that rattles with every movement. Its arms are corded with wiry muscle, and its blunt face is split by a cruel, eager grin. A sling hangs at its side, its pouch filled with slick, yellowed gallstones taken from the hulking Marrow Munchers they bully and drive like beasts. At its hip rests a short, serrated blade, notched and jagged to tear rather than cut. It carries a crude wooden shield painted with a leering face in smeared red ink. It reeks of sweat, metal, and old blood, and fights with grim, workmanlike brutality. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 16 (base 10 + Agility + chain shirt + shield) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 12 (+1) |
| **Constitution** | 12 (+1) |
| **Agility** | 12 (+1) |
| **Intellect** | 8 (-1) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 8 (-1) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Serrated Shortsword:** +3 to hit, range 1, 1d6 + 1 piercing damage. Combat text: *Boggart Bruiser swings a serrated shortsword at (insert target)!* **Sling (Gallstone):** +3 to hit, range 3, 1d6 + 1 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *Boggart Bruiser whips a gallstone from its sling at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | B; **Hooded Lantern** (1%); **1d4 Flasks of Oil** (1%) |
| **Comments** | “Move, you big lump!”, “Hold ‘em down!”, “Break the bones first!”, “Heh—this one’ll bleed nice!”, “Don’t waste the stones!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Bruiser has a chance to drop:
- **Hooded Lantern** — 1%
- **1d4 Flasks of Oil** — 1%

### Hooded Lantern Summary
A battered hooded lantern smeared with soot and greasy fingerprints.
- non-magical

### Flask of Oil Summary
Crude flasks of thick, smoky lamp oil carried for fire-setting and dungeon work.
- non-magical
- quantity: 1d4

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Bruiser has a chance to drop:
- **Hooded Lantern** — 1%
- **1d4 Flasks of Oil** — 1%

### Hooded Lantern Summary
A battered hooded lantern smeared with soot and greasy fingerprints.
- non-magical

### Flask of Oil Summary
Crude flasks of thick, smoky lamp oil carried for fire-setting and dungeon work.
- non-magical
- quantity: 1d4

content.text:
# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Boggart Bruiser](./boggart-bruiser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Armored boggart enforcer with a serrated shortsword, shield, and gallstone sling |
| [Boggart Gasser](./boggart-gasser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Choking boggart bomb-thrower that lobs poisonous gas vials and may carry a **Rotgas Vial** |
| [Boggart Rotwife](./boggart-rotwife.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Filthy boggart crone that spits venom and croons a sleep-inducing **Rotwife’s Lullaby** |
| [Marrow Muncher](./marrow-muncher.md) | 3 | Large | Giant | Reeking brute that smashes foes with an oversized club and hurls skulls from its grisly belt |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

Its wings buzz with a sickening hum, and its distended abdomen pulses faintly with stolen blood. Often kept in crude cages by boggarts, these creatures are released in swarms when intruders are scented, drawn unerringly to fresh blood. |
| **Level** | 2 |

| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |

| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |

| **Damage Immunities** | None |

If hit, the target must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or contract **Veinrot** *(disease; for 1 hour, the target takes an extra 50% damage from all Bleeding effects).* Combat text: *Rotwing stabs (insert target) with its proboscis!* |
| **Treasure Type** | None |
| **Comments** | None |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Disease Note
**Veinrot** is currently described here as:
- for 1 hour, the target takes an extra 50% damage from all Bleeding effects

### Encounter Note
Rotwings are often kept in crude cages by boggarts and released in swarms once prey has been wounded or cornered.

content.text:
# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Boggart Bruiser](./boggart-bruiser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Armored boggart enforcer with a serrated shortsword, shield, and gallstone sling |
| [Boggart Gasser](./boggart-gasser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Choking boggart bomb-thrower that lobs poisonous gas vials and may carry a **Rotgas Vial** |
| [Boggart Rotwife](./boggart-rotwife.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Filthy boggart crone that spits venom and croons a sleep-inducing **Rotwife’s Lullaby** |
| [Marrow Muncher](./marrow-muncher.md) | 3 | Large | Giant | Reeking brute that smashes foes with an oversized club and hurls skulls from its grisly belt |
| [Rotwing](./rotwing.md) | 2 | Small | Monstrosity | Bat-winged blood parasite that causes **Bleeding** and spreads **Veinrot** |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

arguments.content:
# Gloamshadow

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Gloamshadow |
| **Description** | A wavering form born of the gloaming hour, where light fails and shadows deepen. It clings to the edges of vision, barely holding the shape of something once humanoid. Its presence chills the air, frosting breath and dimming flame. Faint whispers coil around it—soft promises of endings yet to come. It drifts rather than walks, drawn to warmth and life, seeking to snuff both out with a deathly touch. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Medium (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Undead |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (base 10 + Agility) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d8 + 2 |
| **Strength** | 6 (-2) |
| **Constitution** | 12 (+1) |
| **Agility** | 12 (+1) |
| **Intellect** | 8 (-1) |
| **Piety** | 10 (+0) |
| **Personality** | 6 (-2) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | Cold |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Weakening Touch:** +3 to hit, range 1, 1d6 cold damage; the target loses **1d3 Strength**. This reduction lasts for **1 hour**. If this effect reduces the target’s Strength to **0**, the target dies. Combat text: *Gloamshadow reaches out with a weakening, freezing touch toward (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | None |
| **Comments** | “The light fades...”, “You weaken...”, “All warmth dies...”, “Come into the dark...”, “You will not last...” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Strength Drain Note
**Weakening Touch** reduces the target’s Strength by **1d3** for **1 hour**.
- if this reduces the target’s Strength to **0**, the target dies

### Undead Note
Gloamshadows drift toward heat, light, and living breath, dimming their surroundings as they close.

content.text:
# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Boggart Bruiser](./boggart-bruiser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Armored boggart enforcer with a serrated shortsword, shield, and gallstone sling |
| [Boggart Gasser](./boggart-gasser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Choking boggart bomb-thrower that lobs poisonous gas vials and may carry a **Rotgas Vial** |
| [Boggart Rotwife](./boggart-rotwife.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Filthy boggart crone that spits venom and croons a sleep-inducing **Rotwife’s Lullaby** |
| [Gloamshadow](./gloamshadow.md) | 2 | Medium | Undead | Wavering undead shade with a freezing touch that drains **Strength** and can kill the helpless |
| [Marrow Muncher](./marrow-muncher.md) | 3 | Large | Giant | Reeking brute that smashes foes with an oversized club and hurls skulls from its grisly belt |
| [Rotwing](./rotwing.md) | 2 | Small | Monstrosity | Bat-winged blood parasite that causes **Bleeding** and spreads **Veinrot** |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

arguments.content:
# Grave Slurry

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Grave Slurry |
| **Description** | A heaving mass of liquefied corpses, grave soil, and splintered bone, slowly dragging itself forward in a wet, sucking crawl. Pale fragments of faces and limbs surface and sink within it, while jagged shards of bone jut outward like broken teeth. The air around it is thick with the stench of rot and churned earth, enough to turn the stomach before it ever strikes. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Medium (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Undead, Ooze |
| **Alignment** | Neutral |
| **AC** | 10 |
| **Hit Points** | 2d8 + 2 |
| **Strength** | 12 (+1) |
| **Constitution** | 12 (+1) |
| **Agility** | 6 (-2) |
| **Intellect** | 1 (-5) |
| **Piety** | 4 (-3) |
| **Personality** | 1 (-5) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Pseudopod:** +3 to hit, range 1, 2d4 acid damage. A successful hit generates a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check, with a **DC of 5** *(see ITEM DESTRUCTION).* Combat text: *Grave Slurry lashes out with a sludge-thick pseudopod toward (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | None |
| **Comments** | Wet gurgling, bone grinding, low sucking noises |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Item Destruction Note
A successful **Pseudopod** hit generates a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check with a **DC of 5**.

### Encounter Note
Grave Slurries drag themselves through crypts, pits, and churned burial earth, fouling the ground as they advance.

Its saliva carries a paralysing toxin, used to incapacitate prey before it is consumed. When it strikes, the chest snaps shut with violent hunger. Occasionally, a pair of gloves can be found within—remains of a thief who reached in and never withdrew their hands.* |

arguments.content:
# Chest Mimic

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Chest Mimic |
| **Description** | A ravenous, shape-shifting horror that lurks in the guise of a wooden chest. Its lid often sits slightly ajar, a glint of coin within to bait the greedy. Up close, the illusion falters—grain becomes flesh, hinges twitch, and the interior is lined with rows of grinding teeth. Its saliva carries a paralysing toxin, used to incapacitate prey before it is consumed. When it strikes, the chest snaps shut with violent hunger. Occasionally, a pair of gloves can be found within—remains of a thief who reached in and never withdrew their hands. |
| **Level** | 3 |
| **Size** | Medium (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Monstrosity |
| **Alignment** | Neutral |
| **AC** | 13 (natural armor) |
| **Hit Points** | 3d8 + 6 |
| **Strength** | 16 (+3) |
| **Constitution** | 14 (+2) |
| **Agility** | 8 (-1) |
| **Intellect** | 5 (-3) |
| **Piety** | 4 (-3) |
| **Personality** | 2 (-4) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Bite:** +5 to hit, range 1, 1d10 + 3 piercing damage; the target must make a **DC 13 Constitution save** or become **Paralyzed**. The target may repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Combat text: *Chest Mimic snaps shut with a crushing, venom-laced bite on (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | 2 x C (roll twice); **Gloves of the Black Hand** (5%) |
| **Comments** | None |
| **Experience** | 100 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Chest Mimic has a chance to drop:
- **Gloves of the Black Hand** — 5%

### Paralysis Note
A creature hit by the Chest Mimic’s **Bite** must make a **DC 13 Constitution save** or become **Paralyzed**.
- the target may repeat the save at the end of each of its turns
- the effect ends on a success

# 📜 Trow


| Category                       | Details                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Name**                       | Trow                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| **Description**                | *A hulking, hunched brute with long, stringy hair hanging in filthy ropes around its face. Its skin is thick, mottled, and slick with grime, stretched over knotted muscle. A jutting jaw lined with rotting, broken teeth gapes beneath small, suspicious eyes. The creature reeks of damp earth, spoiled meat, and old blood, its presence announced by stench before sight. Despite its bulk, it moves with a twitchy, animal wariness, lashing out in sudden bursts of violence.* |
| **Level**                      | 4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| **Size**                       | Large (2 squares on combat grid)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| **Type**                       | Giant                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| **Alignment**                  | Evil                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| **AC**                         | 13 (natural armor)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| **Hit Points**                 | 4d12 + 12                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| **Strength**                   | 18 (+4)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| **Constitution**               | 16 (+3)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| **Agility**                    | 10 (+0)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| **Intellect**                  | 6 (-2)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| **Piety**                      | 6 (-2)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| **Personality**                | 6 (-2)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| **Luck**                       | 10 (+0)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| **Proficiency Bonus**          | +3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Constitution +6                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| **Skill Proficiencies**        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities**     | None                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| **Damage Resistances**         | None                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| **Damage Immunities**          | None                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| **Condition Immunities**       | None                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| **Attacks**                    | **Multiattack:** *The Trow makes two attacks, one with its claws and one with its bite.* **1.** *Trow rakes (insert target) with filthy claws!* **Claw:** +7 to hit, range 1, 1d8 + 4 slashing damage. **2.** *Trow lunges forward and tears at (insert target) with its rotting maw!* **Bite:** +7 to hit, range 1, 1d10 + 4 piercing damage.                                                                                                                                        |
| **Treasure Type**              | C                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| **Comments**                   | *“You taste good in stew…”, “Crunchy little bones…”, “I eat you slow…”, “Break you, then chew…”, “Me hungry!”*                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| **Experience**                 | 200                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

---

## Traits

| Trait         | Effect                                                                                                        |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Regeneration  | The Trow regains 2d4 hit points at the start of its turn                                                      |
| Cringing Fury | If the Trow takes 5 or more damage from a single attack, it becomes Frightened until the end of its next turn |

This template is based on the current approved monster stat block canon and includes a few practical authoring fields for combat implementation.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

# Umberhold Monster Stat Block Canon

This document captures the current approved generic monster stat block for Umberhold.

It defines the baseline fields used to build monster entries.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. Core Monster Fields

| Field | Details |
|---|---|
| Name | Name of the creature |
| Description | Describes the creature in detail, including appearance, odor, demeanor, behavior, and other sensory notes |
| Level | 1-20 |
| Size | Small, Medium, Large, Huge, or Gigantic |
| Type | Beast, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Insect, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, or Undead |
| Alignment | Good, Neutral, or Evil |
| AC | Base 10 when unarmored, modified by Agility, armor, and other effects |
| Hit Points | Determined by size and level |
| Strength | 3-30 |
| Constitution | 3-30 |
| Agility | 3-30 |
| Intellect | 3-30 |
| Piety | 3-30 |
| Personality | 3-30 |
| Luck | 3-30 |
| Proficiency Bonus | Based on level progression |
| Saving Throw Proficiencies | List of proficient saves |
| Skill Proficiencies | List of proficient skills |
| Damage Vulnerabilities | List of damage vulnerabilities |
| Damage Resistances | List of damage resistances |
| Damage Immunities | List of damage immunities |
| Condition Immunities | List of condition immunities |
| Attacks | List of attacks, including to-hit, damage, damage type, and special effects |
| Treasure Type | Treasure category from A-Z, based on treasure tables |
| Comments | Optional creature comments, including a 5% chance at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type |
| Experience | XP awarded to the party for slaying the monster |

---

## 2. Monster Level

Monsters use a **Level** value rather than an external challenge rating style label.

Current monster level range:
- **1-20**

---

## 3. Monster Size

Monster size categories are:
- Small
- Medium
- Large
- Huge
- Gigantic

### Combat Occupancy Mapping
Under Umberhold's current combat system, size maps to enemy rank occupancy as follows:
- Small = 1 slot
- Medium = 1 slot
- Large = 2 slots
- Huge = 3 slots
- Gigantic = 4 slots

---

## 4. Monster Types

The current approved monster types are:
- Beast
- Dragon
- Elemental
- Fey
- Fiend
- Giant
- Humanoid
- Insect
- Monstrosity
- Ooze
- Plant
- Undead

---

## 5. Alignment

The current approved alignment values are:
- Good
- Neutral
- Evil

---

## 6. Armor Class

Monster AC uses a base of **10** when unarmored.

AC may then be modified by:
- armor
- Agility bonus
- magical effects
- other special modifiers

---

## 7. Hit Point Progression by Size

Monster hit points are based on size.

| Size | Hit Dice per Level |
|---|---|
| Small | 1d6 per level |
| Medium | 1d8 per level |
| Large | 1d12 per level |
| Huge | 2d10 per level |
| Gigantic | 2d20 per level |

---

## 8. Core Attributes

All monsters use the same seven core attributes as player characters:
- Strength
- Constitution
- Agility
- Intellect
- Piety
- Personality
- Luck

Each attribute currently ranges from:
- **3-30**

---

## 9. Proficiency Bonus by Level

| Level Range | Proficiency Bonus |
|---|---|
| 1-3 | +2 |
| 4-6 | +3 |
| 7-9 | +4 |
| 10-12 | +5 |
| 13-15 | +6 |
| 16-18 | +7 |
| 19-20 | +8 |

---

## 10. Saving Throw and Skill Proficiencies

Monsters may have:
- saving throw proficiencies
- skill proficiencies

If a monster is proficient in a save or skill, it adds its proficiency bonus to the relevant roll.

---

## 11. Damage and Condition Defenses

Monster entries may include:
- damage vulnerabilities
- damage resistances
- damage immunities
- condition immunities

These should use Umberhold's current damage types and condition list.

---

## 12. Attacks

A monster's attack list should include, at minimum:
- attack name
- to-hit bonus
- damage
- damage type
- range
- any attached effects or conditions

### Attack Range Rule
Every monster attack must have a **range value from 1-3**.

---

## 13. Treasure, Comments, and Experience

### Treasure Type
Each monster may include a **Treasure Type** field from **A-Z**, based on Umberhold's treasure tables.

### Comments
Each monster may include a **Comments** field.

This currently supports flavor such as:
- a **5% chance** at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type

### Experience
Each monster should include an **Experience** value showing how much XP is awarded to the party for slaying it.

The exact XP values by monster level are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

---

## 14. Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- monsters use Level rather than challenge rating
- current monster levels are 1-20
- monsters use the five size categories Small, Medium, Large, Huge, and Gigantic
- Small and Medium occupy 1 combat slot
- Large occupies 2 combat slots
- Huge occupies 3 combat slots
- Gigantic occupies 4 combat slots
- the current monster type list is Beast, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Insect, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, and Undead
- the current alignment values are Good, Neutral, and Evil
- unarmored AC starts at 10
- hit point dice scale by size category
- monsters use the same seven core attributes as player characters
- monster attributes currently range from 3-30
- proficiency bonus follows the current level progression table
- monster entries include a Description field
- monster entries include saving throw proficiencies and skill proficiencies
- monster entries may include damage vulnerabilities, damage resistances, damage immunities, and condition immunities
- monster entries include attacks and XP value
- monster entries may include Treasure Type and Comments

---

## 15. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- whether monster hit points use only size dice or also add Constitution-derived bonuses by level
- whether alignment has any systemic effect beyond lore and spell interaction
- the full canonical skill list used by monsters and characters
- whether monster spellcasters follow player spell slot rules or use bespoke spellcasting packages

---

## 16. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Boggart Boneboiler](./boggart-boneboiler.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Grease-hurling boggart brute with a **Polished Finger Bone** drop |
| [Boggart Bruiser](./boggart-bruiser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Armored boggart enforcer with a serrated shortsword, shield, and gallstone sling |
| [Boggart Gasser](./boggart-gasser.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Choking boggart bomb-thrower that lobs poisonous gas vials and may carry a **Rotgas Vial** |
| [Boggart Rotwife](./boggart-rotwife.md) | 2 | Small | Humanoid | Filthy boggart crone that spits venom and croons a sleep-inducing **Rotwife’s Lullaby** |
| [Chest Mimic](./chest-mimic.md) | 3 | Medium | Monstrosity | False treasure chest predator with a paralyzing bite and a chance to contain **Gloves of the Black Hand** |
| [Gloamshadow](./gloamshadow.md) | 2 | Medium | Undead | Wavering undead shade with a freezing touch that drains **Strength** and can kill the helpless |
| [Grave Slurry](./grave-slurry.md) | 2 | Medium | Undead, Ooze | Heaving corpse-sludge that lashes with acid and threatens **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** |
| [Marrow Muncher](./marrow-muncher.md) | 3 | Large | Giant | Reeking brute that smashes foes with an oversized club and hurls skulls from its grisly belt |
| [Rotwing](./rotwing.md) | 2 | Small | Monstrosity | Bat-winged blood parasite that causes **Bleeding** and spreads **Veinrot** |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

content.text:
# Monster Template

Use this template to create a new Umberhold monster entry.

This template is based on the current approved monster stat block canon and includes a few practical authoring fields for combat implementation.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## Core Identity

- **Name:** 
- **Description:** 
- **Level:** 
- **Size:** Small / Medium / Large / Huge / Gigantic
- **Combat Slot Use:** 
  - Small = 1
  - Medium = 1
  - Large = 2
  - Huge = 3
  - Gigantic = 4
- **Type:** Beast / Dragon / Elemental / Fey / Fiend / Giant / Humanoid / Insect / Monstrosity / Ooze / Plant / Undead
- **Alignment:** Good / Neutral / Evil
- **Lore Notes:** 

---

## Combat Profile

- **AC:** 
- **Hit Points Formula:** 
- **Average Hit Points:** 
- **Movement / Rank Preference:** Front / Rear / Flexible
- **Primary Range:** 1 / 2 / 3
- **Role Tags:** 
  - melee
  - ranged
  - caster
  - brute
  - support
  - ambusher
  - boss
  - other: 
- **AI Profile:** 
- **Initiative Modifier:** 

---

## Attributes

- **Strength:** 
- **Constitution:** 
- **Agility:** 
- **Intellect:** 
- **Piety:** 
- **Personality:** 
- **Luck:** 
- **Proficiency Bonus:** 

---

## Proficiencies

### Saving Throw Proficiencies
- 
- 

### Skill Proficiencies
- 
- 

---

## Offense

### Attack 1
- **Name:** 
- **Attack Type:** Melee / Ranged / Spell / Special
- **To Hit:** 
- **Range:** 1 / 2 / 3
- **Targeting:** 
- **Damage:** 
- **Damage Type:** 
- **Additional Effect:** 

### Attack 2
- **Name:** 
- **Attack Type:** Melee / Ranged / Spell / Special
- **To Hit:** 
- **Range:** 1 / 2 / 3
- **Targeting:** 
- **Damage:** 
- **Damage Type:** 
- **Additional Effect:** 

### Attack 3
- **Name:** 
- **Attack Type:** Melee / Ranged / Spell / Special
- **To Hit:**

- **Damage Type:** 
- **Additional Effect:** 

---

## Defense and Traits

### Damage Resistances

### Damage Immunities

### Condition Immunities
- 

### Traits

| Trait | Effect |
|---|---|
|  |  |
|  |  |

---

## Spellcasting (Optional)

- **Caster:** Yes / No
- **Casting Stat:** 
- **Spell Save DC:** 
- **Known Spells:** 
- **Spellcasting Notes:** 

---

## Encounter Use

- **Encounter Tier:** Common / Elite / Mini-Boss / Boss
- **Region:** 
- **Dungeon / Area:** 
- **Random Encounter Table(s):** 
- **Fixed Encounter(s):** 
- **Recommended Groupings:** 

---

## Rewards

- **Treasure Type:** 
- **Experience:** 
- **Gold Drop:** 
- **Loot Table:** 
- **Special Drop / Unique Drop:** 

---

## Comments

- **Turn Comment Pool:** 
- **Turn Comment Chance:** 5% at the start of its turn

---

## Presentation

- **Portrait / Visual Brief:** 
- **Sound / Voice Notes:** 
- **Death FX Notes:** 
- **Combat Animation Notes:** 

---

## Author Notes

- **Design Purpose:** 
- **What This Monster Teaches the Player:** 
- **Balance Concerns:** 
- **Related Monsters / Variants:** 

---

arguments.oldText:
## 13. Treasure, Comments, and Experience

### Treasure Type
Each monster may include a **Treasure Type** field from **A-Z**, based on Umberhold's treasure tables.

### Comments
Each monster may include a **Comments** field.

This currently supports flavor such as:
- a **5% chance** at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type

### Experience
Each monster should include an **Experience** value showing how much XP is awarded to the party for slaying it.

The exact XP values by monster level are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`
arguments.newText:
## 13. Traits, Treasure, Comments, and Experience

### Traits
Each monster may include a **Traits** section.

This is used for passive, reactive, or always-on abilities such as:
- regeneration
- aura effects
- triggered behaviors
- special weaknesses or compulsions

### Treasure Type
Each monster may include a **Treasure Type** field from **A-Z**, based on Umberhold's treasure tables.

### Comments
Each monster may include a **Comments** field.

This currently supports flavor such as:
- a **5% chance** at the start of its turn to speak a random humorous line appropriate to its creature type

### Experience
Each monster should include an **Experience** value showing how much XP is awarded to the party for slaying it.

The exact XP values by monster level are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

arguments.content:
# Trow

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Trow |
| **Description** | A hulking, hunched brute with long, stringy hair hanging in filthy ropes around its face. Its skin is thick, mottled, and slick with grime, stretched over knotted muscle. A jutting jaw lined with rotting, broken teeth gapes beneath small, suspicious eyes. The creature reeks of damp earth, spoiled meat, and old blood, its presence announced by stench before sight. Despite its bulk, it moves with a twitchy, animal wariness, lashing out in sudden bursts of violence. |
| **Level** | 4 |
| **Size** | Large (2 squares on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Giant |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 13 (natural armor) |
| **Hit Points** | 4d12 + 12 |
| **Strength** | 18 (+4) |
| **Constitution** | 16 (+3) |
| **Agility** | 10 (+0) |
| **Intellect** | 6 (-2) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 6 (-2) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +3 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Constitution +6 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Multiattack:** The Trow makes two attacks, one with its claws and one with its bite. **Claw:** +7 to hit, range 1, 1d8 + 4 slashing damage. Combat text: *Trow rakes (insert target) with filthy claws!* **Bite:** +7 to hit, range 1, 1d10 + 4 piercing damage. Combat text: *Trow lunges forward and tears at (insert target) with its rotting maw!* |
| **Treasure Type** | C |
| **Comments** | “You taste good in stew...”, “Crunchy little bones...”, “I eat you slow...”, “Break you, then chew...”, “Me hungry!” |
| **Experience** | 200 |

---

## Traits

| Trait | Effect |
|---|---|
| **Regeneration** | The Trow regains 2d4 hit points at the start of its turn. |
| **Cringing Fury** | If the Trow takes 5 or more damage from a single attack, it becomes **Frightened** until the end of its next turn. |
